blocks.body: Sentence Transformations — Articles / Quantifiers. Keep article choice and quantity natural when countable and uncountable meaning shifts.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"In these transformations, tiny forms do the real work: a / an / the / zero article, plus whether the noun is countable or uncountable. One small change can shift the whole meaning.","rules":[{"label":"COUNTABLE OR UNCOUNTABLE","color":"emerald","text":"Before changing the quantifier, decide what kind of noun you have. That controls many / much, few / little, a few / a little.","example":"few chairs / little time"},{"label":"ARTICLE SIGNALS REFERENCE","color":"blue","text":"During the rewrite, check whether you are talking about one example of a class, a specific known thing, or a general meaning.","example":"a teacher / the teacher / teachers"},{"label":"DON'T LOSE THE QUANTITY","color":"amber","text":"Pairs such as not many -> only a few and not much -> only a little work only when the same quantity idea stays in place.","example":"We have only a few options."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. How to Spot the Needed Rewrite
blocks.body:
In these tasks, check the noun type before you look for the new structure.

  • If the noun is countable singular, you often need an article or another determiner.
  • If the noun is uncountable, you cannot automatically add a / an or many.
  • The pairs few / a few and little / a little differ not only in form but also in attitude: negative shortage vs a small but enough amount.
  • Transformation tasks often test whether you can keep the same meaning through forms such as only a few, very little, no, any, or a piece of.

blocks.heading: 2. Core Transformation Patterns
blocks.body:
Base sentenceTarget sentenceRule focus
There aren't many chairs left.There are only a few chairs left.not many -> only a few for countable plural.
We have very little time.We don't have much time.little -> not much for an uncountable noun.
I didn't see any mistakes.I saw no mistakes.not any -> no without changing the meaning.
She gave me some useful advice.She gave me a useful piece of advice.uncountable noun -> partitive structure.

In article and quantifier transformations, success often depends on one small form rather than on a complex sentence pattern.

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Using much with a countable noun.","wrong":"We don't have much chairs.","right":"We don't have many chairs.","hint":"Much works with uncountable nouns, while many works with countable plural nouns."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Confusing few and a few.","wrong":"We have few options, so we can choose freely.","right":"We have a few options, so we can choose freely.","hint":"Few suggests shortage, while a few suggests a small but useful number."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Adding an article to an uncountable noun.","wrong":"She gave me an advice.","right":"She gave me some advice. / She gave me a piece of advice.","hint":"Advice, information, furniture do not normally take a / an."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"rose","title":"Changing the quantity shade during the rewrite.","wrong":"There is little water left. -> There is a little water left.","right":"Check that the new sentence still shows the same shortage or sufficiency.","hint":"One small a changes the attitude completely."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick Exam Check","items":["First decide whether the noun is countable or uncountable.","Then check what quantity shade must stay the same: not enough or a small but enough amount.","If an article appears, think about new reference, known reference, or general meaning.","Before finishing, reread only the noun phrase: that is where the hidden mistake often sits."]}
